free_text: Evaluated activation cross sections of longer-lived
radionuclides produced by deuteron-induced reactions
on natural nickel
CGR 560 cyclotron
Cyclone-110 cyclotron
High purity, >99.5%, natural Ni foils, from Goodfellow
thickness: 15.5, 23.9, 26.4 microns
Stacks of Ni target and Al and Ti monitor foils were
irradiated with 20.4, 30 and 50 MeV deuteron beams.
Al and Ti foils were used for energy degradation and
for checking the intensity and energy along the stack.
The stacks were irradiated in a Faraday-cup type target
holder equipped with a long collimator and electron
suppressor for charge collection.
Measurements started 30 min after EOB. The short lived
radionuclides were not assed.
The count rate was kept low so that corrections
for pile up effects and dead time could be implemented
through the acquisition and analyzing software.
The charge on target was first derived from the
Faraday-cup measurement and was adjusted according to
the monitor reactions.
Chapter 4
Decay data were taken from NUDAT database.
The uncertainty of the primary beam energy was
0.3 MeV. The uncertainty on the average energy of the
last foil of a long stack, due to the uncertainty on
the foil thickness and to energy degradation was
estimated to be 0.9 MeV.
For estimation of standard uncertainty on the
cross section values the individual uncertainties
included in the propagated error are:
Number of target nuclei including
non-uniformity (5%),
incident particle intensity (4-6%),
detection efficiency (3-7%) depending
on the energy
peak areas incl. statistical unc. (1-9%),
gamma decay data (1-5%).
The total uncertainty of 8-15% is obtained as
positive square root of a sum of the contributing
sources in quadrature.
The non-linear effects of the possible uncertainty of
the half-lives were not taken into account.
The measured experimental data were compared
with earlier literature values.
Recommended data were given from threshold
up to 50 MeV.
B.Kiraly, 2007.
